# RemediationReport — Agent Integration Guide

> Machine-readable instructions for AI agents working inside this package. Humans should read [`./README.md`](./README.md) first.

## What this package is

**This package is the UI of the shipped remediation report** (record 0057). It is no longer a preview twin.

`src/report/` is the report itself: React renders every surface of it, Vite compiles that tree — with React compiled *in* — into three self-contained artifacts, and those are committed into `../langgraph-repo-remediation-html-report-generator/vendor/`, which embeds them in one HTML page. The generator is now purely the DATA layer (`buildReportData(channels) → ReportData`); it authors no markup, CSS, or script.

> **Superseded.** This file used to say *"It is the React twin of the static renderer. **Do not merge them.** … a React toolchain would break all three of its invariants."* That is now false. React's dependencies are needed at BUILD time, never at RUNTIME — a committed bundle keeps every one of the generator's invariants (zero deps, offline tests, no external asset) while ending the duplication. The old separation had already cost real correctness: the two UIs drifted until they had **disjoint `data-testid` vocabularies**, and two of record 0056's three defect fixes were structurally inexpressible in this package's old contract.

**If you change `src/report/`, you MUST run `npm run build:bundle`** to rebuild and republish the committed artifacts. The harness `verify` gate runs `build:bundle -- --check` and fails on a stale bundle.

Three rules govern `src/report/` and each one has teeth:

1. **Plain global class names, never CSS Modules.** `behavior.test.mjs` and the pack's matrix tests assert on literal markup (`class="chip chip-out"`, `data-testid="repo-card"`). CSS Modules hash their class names and would break every one of those gates. `src/report/report.css` is a plain global stylesheet on purpose.
2. **Never move navigation into React state.** Every view, repo detail, and tab panel must be in the prerendered markup, with visibility driven by CSS `:checked` radios (`src/report/navCss.ts`). The "obvious" `{open ? <Detail/> : <Overview/>}` refactor silently costs the report its JS-disabled readability *and* breaks the generator's `behavior.test.mjs`, which slices five different repo cards out of the emitted HTML. `Report.test.tsx` fails if you try.
3. **Hydration must not mismatch.** The committed bundle is a production React build, and production React *silences* mismatch warnings — so a mismatch would ship as a subtly wrong page with green CI. `Report.test.tsx` hydrates the prerender and fails on any React warning.

The `atoms/`, `molecules/`, and `organisms/RemediationReport/` trees are the **original design mock**. They do NOT ship and they are not the report. They keep their examples, stories, and tests — but on their **own page** (`/mock/`), never on the report's.

## The dev server renders the shipped report

`http://localhost:5247` mounts `src/report/Report.tsx` over the `ReportData` of a report the flow actually emitted. Same tree as the page, same data as the page, same stylesheet as the page.

**It did not always.** Record 0057 unified the *shipped* renderer on React but left `dev/` mounting `organisms/RemediationReport` over `SAMPLE_REPOS` — the design mock over invented data — so the dev server and `repo-remediation.html` visibly disagreed, which is the exact drift 0057 exists to prevent. Two things now hold it shut, and both are cheap to break by accident:

- **`dev/` imports no stylesheet.** The emitted page inlines `src/report/report.css` and nothing else. The old harness loaded Tailwind, whose preflight alone restyles headings, buttons, and borders — a dev server showing a report the reader never gets. Do not add a global stylesheet here.
- **`#root` holds only `<Report/>`.** The session picker is portaled into a separate `#devbar` node, exactly as the emitted page keeps everything but the report out of `#root`.

Data comes from [`dev/sessions.mjs`](./dev/sessions.mjs), which reads the `<script type="application/json" id="report-data">` island back out of an emitted page. That island *is* `buildReportData(channels)`'s return value (record 0057/D2), so it is not a reconstruction of the shipped data — it is the shipped data.

### Two pages, and the split is load-bearing

| URL | What it renders | Styling |
|---|---|---|
| `/` | **the report** — `src/report/` over real session data | `src/report/report.css` alone |
| `/mock/` | **the design mock** — `organisms/RemediationReport` over the *same* data, adapted | Tailwind |

### One example, one URL

Every example is addressable, so it can be linked, bookmarked, and reloaded:

| URL | Example |
|---|---|
| `/` → `/as-run` | the session exactly as the flow emitted it (`/` canonicalizes to `/as-run`) |
| `/blocked` | the same repos, every outcome blocked — pass rate is an em-dash, not `0%` |
| `/empty` | the same dataset, zero repositories ingested |
| `/mock/` `/mock/empty` `/mock/blocked` `/mock/hidden` | the design mock's four examples |

Add `?session=<id>` to pick WHICH emitted run supplies the data. The split is deliberate: the example is a fact about the report and belongs in the path; the session is a fact about this machine's disk, and a URL naming a session id is not one anyone else can open.

[`dev/routes.ts`](./dev/routes.ts) owns the grammar, and `dev/routes.test.ts` pins it — including the case that already bit once, where `/?variant=blocked` (a link from the old query-param scheme) silently rendered `as-run`. The report's paths are derived from `VARIANTS`, so **a new variant gets its URL for free**.

The `harness:example-routes` middleware in [`vite.config.ts`](./vite.config.ts) is what makes a deep path survive a reload: it maps the report's paths to `dev/index.html` and anything under `/mock/` to `dev/mock/index.html`. **The mock's `BrowserRouter` depends on it.** Vite's own SPA fallback answers *every* unmatched HTML request with the ROOT `index.html` — so without that middleware, `/mock/blocked` quietly serves the *report*, which is why the mock used to hide behind a `HashRouter`. Remove the middleware and the hash router has to come back with it.

They are **separate documents**, not routes of one app, and that is the only isolation that actually holds. Tailwind's preflight is a global reset: import it anywhere in the report's page and it restyles the report, no matter how the components are organized. A separate document is what drops the stylesheet. Do not "unify" these two pages into one router.

The mock's harness is [`dev/mock/main.tsx`](./dev/mock/main.tsx) (HashRouter — vite's dev server would otherwise fall back to the ROOT `index.html` for a deep path like `/mock/empty` and serve the *report* where a mock example was asked for).

### One dataset, several variants — no fixture is reachable from either page

[`dev/variants.ts`](./dev/variants.ts) holds pure `ReportData → ReportData` transforms of the real emitted session, and **every example on both pages is one of them**:

| variant | what it is |
|---|---|
| `as-run` | the session exactly as the flow emitted it |
| `blocked` | the same repos with every outcome blocked — pass rate is an em-dash, **never `0%`** (records 0033, 0054) |
| `empty` | the same dataset, zero repos ingested |

Totals are **recomputed** (`recount`), never hand-written, so a derived run is as internally consistent as a captured one. The report's picker crosses sessions × variants; the mock's four routes are `as-run`, `blocked`, `empty`, and `as-run` + flag-off.

The mock cannot consume `ReportData` (it predates it), so [`dev/mock/adapt.ts`](./dev/mock/adapt.ts) maps one to the other. **Read that file's mappings as a bug report, not as plumbing** — every lossy line is something the real report expresses and the mock cannot (`Verdict` vs `Outcome`, the ledger map vs a single scalar, `severity: 'unknown'`). That gap is what "two UIs" costs, made explicit.

When no report is on disk, **both pages say what to run instead of falling back to a fixture.** A fixture that looks like a report is how this harness drifted from the shipped page in the first place. `fixtures.ts` still exists — for the mock's stories and unit tests, where invented data is the right tool.

## Quick start

```bash
make up     # install + start vite (background)
make logs   # tail the dev log
make down   # stop

npm run check:report          # does this package still render the emitted pages? (see below)
```

Dev URL: `http://localhost:5247` — see [`Makefile`](./Makefile) and [`vite.config.ts`](./vite.config.ts).

By default it renders the newest report under `../../.harness/<session-id>/repo-remediation.html`, and the picker switches between sessions. Override with `HARNESS_REPORT_HTML=<file>` (one report) or `HARNESS_SESSIONS_DIR=<dir>` (a directory of them). No sessions on disk yet? The page says so and tells you what to run — it does not fall back to fixtures, because a fixture that looks like a report is how this drifted in the first place.

## `npm run check:report` — the parity gate

[`scripts/check-report.mjs`](./scripts/check-report.mjs) proves the claim rather than asserting it: it reads an emitted page's island, re-renders it through the **committed bundle**, and requires the result to be byte-identical to the page's prerendered `#root`. Run it against one file or (with no argument) every session on disk.

Pair it with `npm run build:bundle -- --check` and the chain is closed end to end: `src/report/` → committed bundle → emitted page. A `check:report` failure means the page on disk was written by a different tree than the one in `src/report/` — a stale report, or a stale bundle.

## Layout (authoritative)

- [`src/report/`](./src/report/) — **the report.** `Report.tsx` is the composition root; `entry-server.tsx` / `entry-client.tsx` are the two halves of the committed bundle; `types.ts` is the data contract (the TS mirror of the generator's `buildReportData`); `report.css` is the only stylesheet that ships.
- [`dev/`](./dev/) — the report's dev harness. `main.tsx` mounts `src/report/` over real session data; `sessions.mjs` finds and reads it; `variants.ts` derives the run states; `index.html` carries dev chrome only (no stylesheet).
- [`dev/mock/`](./dev/mock/) — the design mock's dev harness, a **separate page** at `/mock/`: its own `index.html`, its own `main.tsx`, its own `styles.css` (the only Tailwind in the package), `data.ts` + `adapt.ts` (the same real data, mapped to the mock's older contract), and the four `example-*.tsx` reference integrations.
- [`scripts/build-bundle.mjs`](./scripts/build-bundle.mjs) — build + publish the committed bundle. [`scripts/check-report.mjs`](./scripts/check-report.mjs) — assert an emitted page came from this tree.
- [`src/index.ts`](./src/index.ts) — public barrel; consumers import from here.
- [`src/organisms/RemediationReport/`](./src/organisms/RemediationReport/) — the design mock's organism. **Not the report.**
  - `RemediationReport.tsx` — composition root; owns the outcome filter + selected repo.
  - `parts/` — organism-private sub-components. **Never re-exported.**
  - `fixtures.ts` — sample repos for stories, tests, and `dev/`.
  - `RemediationReport.stories.tsx` — includes a `Hidden` story with the flag off.
  - `RemediationReport.test.tsx` — includes a `returns null when flag is off` case.
  - [`README.md`](./src/organisms/RemediationReport/README.md) — human-facing usage doc.
- [`src/atoms/`](./src/atoms/), [`src/molecules/`](./src/molecules/) — package-local; never shared across sibling packages.
- [`src/featureFlags/`](./src/featureFlags/) — package-local primitive used by the mock's components.

## Public API

The report is consumed as a **committed bundle**, not as an import — see `../langgraph-repo-remediation-html-report-generator/src/bundle.mjs`. There is no runtime dependency on this package.

```ts
// The two entry points Vite compiles. Nothing else is a shipping surface.
renderReport(data: ReportData): string   // src/report/entry-server.tsx  → vendor/report-ssr.mjs
                                         // src/report/entry-client.tsx  → vendor/report-client.js (hydrates #root)
```

The design mock is still exported from [`src/index.ts`](./src/index.ts) (`RemediationReport`, atoms, molecules, feature flags), still tested, and still previewable at `/mock/` — but nothing ships it, and it is never what `/` renders.

## Invariants (do not break)

- **`/` renders the shipped tree over shipped data.** Never point the report's page back at the mock or at fixtures; a dev server that renders something the reader will not get is worse than no dev server.
- **Every example is a variant of a real emitted session.** New example? Add a transform to `dev/variants.ts`, don't hand-write a dataset. No fixture fallback on either page — that fallback *is* the drift.
- **Tailwind lives on `/mock/` and nowhere else.** No stylesheet is imported in `dev/main.tsx` or `dev/index.html`; the emitted page carries `report.css` alone. Never merge the two pages into one router — the separate document is the isolation.
- **Rebuild the bundle after any `src/report/` change** (`npm run build:bundle`), or the emitted page and this tree drift apart. `check:report` catches it; `build:bundle -- --check` catches it earlier.
- **No upward imports** in the mock: `atoms/` never imports `molecules/` or `organisms/`.
- **`parts/` stays private.** Never re-exported from `src/index.ts`.
- **Pass rate excludes blocked and skipped.** `fixed / (fixed + broken + bug)` — a repo blocked by a dead registry is not a remediation failure (record 0033).
- **Never make this a `harness` workspace member.** Its deps would reach the dependency-free generator.

## Where to look first

| Question | File |
|----------|------|
| What does the shipped report render? | `src/report/Report.tsx` |
| What is the data contract? | `src/report/types.ts` (mirrors the generator's `src/data.mjs`) |
| Where does the dev server get real data? | `dev/sessions.mjs` |
| Does an emitted page match this tree? | `npm run check:report` → `scripts/check-report.mjs` |
| What does a valid mock consumer call look like? | `dev/mock/example-default.tsx` (served at `/mock/`) |
| What behaviors are under test? | `src/report/Report.test.tsx` (+ the generator's `behavior.test.mjs`) |
| Human-facing docs? | `./README.md` |
